DTR  |  Listen to a Classroom Conversation
LCC-HS10-L1-BR300-HSELA910-MFJ-IMR-01 • HS English/ELA Grades 9–10 • Analyzing How Authors Transform Source Material

The Author's Call

Click  🎧  to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer some questions.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1: What does the girl ask the boy for help with?
2QUESTION 2: Why does the boy say the author made the main character younger than the real person?
3QUESTION 3: By the end of the conversation, what does the girl understand about what her journal should do?
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
Answer all 3 questions to submit.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Listen again. Say one thing the girl says and one thing the boy says out loud.
CA ELD PI.A.1, PI.A.3, PI.B.5  |  CCSS.ELA-LITERACY.RL.9-10.9  |  HS English/ELA Grades 9-10
